# `liber:device@1`

Generated reference for the `liber:device` package (`device.lsidl`). See [the LSIDL language](../../../LSIDL.md).

LiberSystem device package - device and USB enumeration services.

## Imports

- `error` from [`liber:base@1`](/docs/gen/liber/base/v1.md)

## Types

### enum `device-type`

The class of a discovered device, mirroring the kernel's device type codes.

| case | ordinal | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `unknown` | 0 |  |
| `net` | 1 |  |
| `block` | 2 |  |
| `console` | 3 |  |
| `usb` | 4 |  |

### record `device-entry`

One discovered device: its kernel-table index, class, and the size of the MMIO window its driver maps.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `index` | `u32` |  |
| `type` | `device-type` |  |
| `mmio-len` | `u64` |  |

### record `usb-device`

One enumerated USB device, as the xHCI driver addressed it: the root port it hangs off (a device behind a hub reports the hub's root port), its speed, the vendor and product ids from its device descriptor, its class code, and the role the driver bound it to ("hub", "keyboard", "storage", or "device" for an unclaimed one).

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `port` | `u32` |  |
| `speed` | `string` |  |
| `vendor` | `u32` |  |
| `product` | `u32` |  |
| `class` | `u32` |  |
| `type` | `string` |  |

## Interfaces

### interface `device`

The Device service contract: enumerate the devices the kernel discovered on the bus, or look one up by its table index. Typed enumeration over the kernel device table (the same table DeviceManager binds drivers to).

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `list` | `list() -> result<list<device-entry>, error>` |  |
| 2 | `get` | `get(index: u32) -> result<device-entry, error>` |  |

### interface `usb`

The USB bus contract, served by the xHCI driver itself (the owner of the bus state): enumerate the devices it addressed, live - hot-plugged and detached devices come and go from the list. The `lsusb` inventory reads this.

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `list` | `list() -> result<list<usb-device>, error>` |  |
